# memory-sentry

> A Claude Code skill for proactive memory monitoring and cleanup on macOS.

## What this skill does
Diagnoses and fixes memory bloat in Claude Code sessions on macOS.
Monitors memory pressure, identifies orphan VS Code Helper processes,
and guides safe cleanup in the correct order.

## When to use this skill
- Claude Code shows "High memory usage (X GB) · /heapdump"
- VS Code Helper processes are consuming excessive RAM
- Mac feels slow during a Claude Code session
- You want to run /compact, sudo purge, or kill orphan processes
- You need a system memory health check

## Files
- SKILL.md — full instructions Claude follows
- scripts/check_pressure.sh — system memory health report
- scripts/find_orphans.sh — list stale VS Code Helper processes
- scripts/kill_orphans.sh — safely terminate orphan helpers
- scripts/watch_memory.sh — background monitor with alerts
- install.sh — one-time setup (chmod +x all scripts)

## Install
npx skills add Mohammed21Sharraf/memory-sentry

## Requirements
macOS 12+, bash, sysctl, ps, pkill, vm_stat, bc (all built into macOS)
